info.title: AI Force: Prompt Engineering Service(PES)
info.description:
The AI Force: Prompt Engineering Service (PES) API provides endpoints 
    for managing prompts, including saving, updating, deleting, listing, 
    and retrieving prompt details. It supports functionalities such as prompt generation, 
    testing, evaluation, and execution. The API is designed to handle advanced configurations, 
    project-specific prompts, and metadata, ensuring efficient prompt management and 
    integration with Large Language Models (LLMs). 
    Authentication is enforced using HTTP Bearer tokens, and health check endpoints 
    are included for monitoring service availability.

post.description:
Create a new prompt in the database.

    Endpoint:
        POST /prompt_studio/save_prompt

    Args:
        request (Request): FastAPI request object.
        name (str): Name of the prompt (required).
        description (str, optional): Description of the prompt.
        system_prompt (str, optional): System prompt text.
        user_prompt (str): User prompt text (required).
        examples (str, optional): JSON string of example items.
        lm_config_id (int): Language model config ID (required).
        lm_params (str, optional): JSON string of advanced LM parameters.
        mcp_enabled (bool, optional): Flag to enable MCP.
        is_public (bool, optional): Flag for public/private collection.
        publish_status (bool): Publish status (required).
        version (str, optional): Prompt version.
        parent_prompt_id (int, optional): Parent prompt ID.
        evaluation (str, optional): JSON string of evaluation details.
        varriables (str, optional): JSON string of variables.
        service (PromptStudioService): Dependency-injected service instance.

    Returns:
        BaseResponse: JSON response indicating success and containing the saved prompt data.

put.description:
Update an existing prompt in the database.

    Args:
        prompt_id (int): The unique identifier of the prompt to be updated.
        request (PromptDetailsUpdateRequest):
            The request body containing the updated details of the prompt.
        service (PromptStudioService):
            The service instance used to handle the prompt updating logic.

    Returns:
        BaseResponse: A JSON response indicating the success of the operation,
                        along with the updated prompt data.

delete.description:
Delete an existing prompt from the database.

    Args:
        request (PromptDetailsDeleteRequest):
            The request body containing the details of the prompt to be deleted.
        service (PromptStudioService):
            The service instance used to handle the prompt deletion logic.

    Returns:
        BaseResponse: A JSON response indicating the success of the operation,
                        along with the details of the deleted prompt.

get.description:
Handle list prompt request.

    Args:
        request (dict): The request body containing project id  details.
    Returns:
        BaseResponse: JSON response with the prompt result

parameters.name: page_size

schema.description: Number of items per page, if zero then all records will be fetched
schema.title: Page Size

parameters.description: Number of items per page, if zero then all records will be fetched

get.description:
Handle get prompt request.
    Args:
        request (dict): The request body containing project id  details.
    Returns:
        BaseResponse: JSON response with the prompt result.

post.description:
Generate a new prompt based on the provided details.

    Args:
        request (PromptDetailsSaveRequest):
            The request body containing the details for the new prompt.
        service (PromptStudioService):
            The service instance used to handle the prompt generation logic.

    Returns:
        BaseResponse: A JSON response indicating the success of the operation,
                        along with the generated prompt details.

Test a prompt using the provided details.

    Endpoint:
        POST /prompt_studio/test_prompt

    Args:
        request (Request): FastAPI request object.
        system_prompt (str, optional): System prompt text.
        user_prompt (str): User prompt text (required).
        examples (str, optional): JSON string of example items.
        lm_config_id (int): Language model config ID (required).
        lm_params (str, optional): JSON string of advanced LM parameters.
        mcp_enabled (bool, optional): Flag to enable MCP.
        varriables (str, optional): JSON string of variables.
        promptId (int): Prompt ID to test the prompt.
        service (PromptStudioService): Dependency-injected service instance.

    Returns:
        BaseResponse: JSON response with the test prompt result.

Handle user login request.
    Args:
        request (dict): The request body containing user login details.
    Returns:
        BaseResponse: JSON response with the login result.

get.description:
Fetch available metrics from the GRC config API, filtered by applicability,
state, custom, and LLM-based flag.
Requires a valid Bearer token in the Authorization header.

Retrieves a list of all datasets for the prompt studio only.
use_case_type is always enforced as 'prompt' for this endpoint, regardless of incoming value.
Returns dataset id, name, description, and created timestamp.

Fetch trace logs for a given trace ID.
- **trace_id**: The trace ID to fetch logs for.
Returns the trace logs or a not found error.
Poll compliance scan status every 5 seconds until a final response is received.

## Perform a Health Check
Returns:
    HealthCheck: Returns a JSON response with the health status

format.title: Format
format.description: Expected response format. Can be 'json' or 'text'.

AdvanceParameters.title: AdvanceParameters
AdvanceParameters.description:
Schema for advanced LLM parameters used during testing.
Includes settings like temperature, token limits, and response format.

examples.title: Examples
examples.description: JSON string of ExampleItem list, e.g. [{"input":"", "output":""}]

lm_params.title: Lm Params
lm_params.description: JSON string of advanced parameters, e.g. {"temperature":0.7,"max_token":2000}

trace_id.title: Trace Id
trace_id.description: Trace: to record for llm call e.g {"status": "","trace_id":""}

evaluation.title: Evaluation
evaluation.description: JSON string of evaluation details, e.g. {"choosed_evaluators": {"PII Evaluator (Input)": false, "Prompt Injection Evaluator": false, "Secrets Evaluator (Input)": true, "Toxicity Evaluator (Input)": false, "Prompt Clarity Evaluator": false}, "selected_test_data": {"Secrets Evaluator (Input)": "DataTest"}, "evaluation_metrics": [{"metric": "Secrets Evaluator (Input)", "score": 1.0, "is_pass": true, "threshold": 0.5}]}

use_case_type.title: Use Case Type
use_case_type.description: Use case type is prompt here

ExampleItem.title: ExampleItem
ExampleItem.description:
Schema for example items.
Represents input-output examples for prompts.

prompt_id.title: Prompt Id
prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to be executed.

data.title: Data
data.description: Optional data to be used with the prompt. In case of additional prompt data, pass the value in `__global_var_prompt_data__` key.

config_id.title: Config Id
config_id.description: ID of the model configuration to be used.

llm_guard_enabled.title: Llm Guard Enabled
llm_guard_enabled.description: Flag to enable or disable LLM guard checks.

ExecutePromptRequest.title: ExecutePromptRequest
ExecutePromptRequest.description:
Represents the request structure for executing a prompt.

Attributes:
    prompt (str): The prompt to be executed.
    user (EmbeddingUserParams): User parameters associated with the request.

HealthCheck.title: HealthCheck
HealthCheck.description: Response model to validate and return when performing a health check.

dataset_items.title: Dataset Items
dataset_items.description: If provided, use these items instead of dataset. Each item will be sanitized.

input_prompt.title: Input Prompt
input_prompt.example: Write a detailed story involving a person named {{Name}} who shares his SSN {SSN}.

system_prompt.title: System Prompt
system_prompt.example: You are a helpful assistant that ensures all user inputs are anonymized before responding.

metric_datasets.title: Metric Datasets
metric_datasets.description: List of metric-dataset pairs or metric-dataset_items objects.

dataset_items.actual_output: In the small misty village of Eldergrove nestled deep within the ancient Whispering Woods there lived a young girl named Elara Eldergrove was a place where magic lingered in the air and the trees seemed to hum with secrets of old The villagers were simple folk tending to their farms and living in harmony with the enchanted forest that surrounded them

prompt_id.title: Prompt Id
prompt_id.description: Unique identifier for the prompt to be deleted.

evaluation_id.title: Evaluation Id
evaluation_id.description: Evaluation ID associated with the prompt.

PromptDetailsDeleteRequest.title: PromptDetailsDeleteRequest
PromptDetailsDeleteRequest.description:
Schema for prompt details deletion request.
Used to delete a prompt based on its ID.

is_deleted.title: Is Deleted
is_deleted.description: Indicates whether the prompt was successfully deleted.

PromptDetailsDeleteResponse.title: PromptDetailsDeleteResponse
PromptDetailsDeleteResponse.description:
Schema for prompt details deletion response.
Represents the response after deleting a prompt, indicating success.

PromptDetailsListResponse.title: PromptDetailsListResponse
PromptDetailsListResponse.description:
Schema for prompt details list response.
Contains the list of prompt items and the total count.

PromptDetailsSaveResponse.title: PromptDetailsSaveResponse
PromptDetailsSaveResponse.description:
Schema for prompt details save response.
Represents the response after saving a prompt, including its ID.

prompt_id.title: Prompt Id
prompt_id.description: Unique identifier for the updated prompt.

PromptDetailsUpdateResponse.title: PromptDetailsUpdateResponse
PromptDetailsUpdateResponse.description:
Schema for prompt details update response.
Represents the response after updating a prompt, including its ID.

PromptExecutionResponse.title: PromptExecutionResponse
PromptExecutionResponse.description:
Schema for prompt execution response.
Represents the response after executing a prompt.

framework.title: Framework
framework.description:
Framework to use for prompt generation.

Supported values:
- `Free-Form` → Free-form prompting.
- `ReAct` → Reason + Act (DSPy)
- `COT` → Chain of Thought reasoning.
- `MCOT` → Multi-chain comparison.
- `Reflection` → Self-reflective refinement.
- `RISEN` → Iterative self-enhancement.
- `RTF` → Role-Task-Format prompting.
- `Self-Consistency` → Averages multiple reasoning chains.

PromptGenerateRequest.title: PromptGenerateRequest
PromptGenerateRequest.description:
Schema for generating a prompt.
Used to generate a prompt based on user input and configuration.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project identifier associated with the prompt.

lm_params.description: Language model parameters for the prompt.

is_public.title: Is Public
is_public.description: Indicates if the prompt is public.

PromptListItem.title: PromptListItem
PromptListItem.description:
Schema for a prompt list item.
Represents a summary of a prompt with its name, description, and metadata.

response.title: Response
response.description: List of responses generated during prompt testing.

PromptTestResponse.title: PromptTestResponse
PromptTestResponse.description:
Schema for prompt testing response.
Represents the response after testing a prompt.

Prompts.title: Prompts
Prompts.description: Model representing a prompt in the system.

isFileInput.title: Isfileinput
isFileInput.description: Indicates if the variable is a file input.

isRequired.title: Isrequired
isRequired.description: Indicates if the variable is required.

fileName.title: Filename
fileName.description: File name associated with the variable.

VariableItems.title: VariableItems
VariableItems.description:
Schema for variable items.
